---
title: "CodeBook Studio"
date: "2026"
date-format: "YYYY"
abstract: "A browser-based annotation tool for computational social science. Define a codebook, annotate texts, adjudicate disagreements, and export labelled data for LLM benchmarking in CodeBook Lab."
hide-abstract: true
image: "../images/codebook-studio.png"
applink: "https://codebook.streamlit.app/"
docslink: "/codebook-lab/studio.html"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: article
    template-partials:
      - "../partials/title-block.html"
---

CodeBook Studio is a browser-based text annotation tool for computational social science. Researchers define an annotation task once — with sections, instructions, examples, tooltips, and response formats — annotate texts in the browser, adjudicate unresolved coder disagreements, and export the codebook and labelled data for downstream use, including systematic LLM benchmarking in [CodeBook Lab](codebook-lab.qmd).

- Checkbox, dropdown, Likert-scale, textbox, and span-style annotation types
- Codebook editor with integrated instructions, examples, and live annotation preview
- Consistent annotation interface for teams of coders
- Codebook export in JSON, LaTeX, and Markdown for papers and appendices
- Adjudication mode for completing unresolved labels from CodeBook Lab queues
- Version comparison for checking changes between codebook drafts
- LLM prompt preview for checking how the task will be presented to models
- CSV input and output for interoperability

For a full guide to CodeBook Studio's features, see the [CodeBook Studio documentation](../codebook-lab/studio.html).

The app is available at [codebook.streamlit.app](https://codebook.streamlit.app/). Citation details for CodeBook Studio are available on the [CodeBook citation page](../codebook-lab/citation.html).
